HUNTINGTON HEALTH CARE & REHABILITATION CENTER

CCN: 676183 · HUNTINGTON, TX 75949 · Angelina County

Overview

Address
220 E ASH STREET, HUNTINGTON, TX 75949
Phone
9368762273
Certified beds
112
Avg daily residents
63 (56% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit partnership
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2008-07-21
Setting
Rural
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.67 3.49 4.06 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.18 0.61 0.20 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.98 0.77 1.08 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.51 2.11 2.77 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.16 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.32
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.14
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.67
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
44.4%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.24
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.90
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-07-16 14 13 1 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-05-30 14 14 0 108 1 108
Total weighted health score
78.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (40)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
39 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2025-07-16 2025-07-17
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2025-04-03 2025-04-04
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. F 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0848 Provide a neutral and fair arbitration process and agree to arbitrator and venue. F 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0926 Have policies on smoking. F 2024-05-30 2024-05-31
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0839 Employ staff that are licensed, certified, or registered in accordance with state laws. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. C 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2023-03-30 2023-03-31
